zh.lifeSummary: 當Judith Smith於大約1636年出生在英國時，她的父親Thomas Smith40歲，母親Margaret Clarke35歲。她於1656年5月3日與Richard Tozer在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1683年6月18日在美國緬因州約克貝維其可享年48歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普洛文斯 本 曼恩約克。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Judith Smith于大约1636年出生在英国时，她的父亲Thomas Smith40岁，母亲Margaret Clarke35岁。她于1656年5月3日与Richard Tozer在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1683年6月18日在美国缅因州約克貝維其可享年48岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普洛文斯 本 曼恩約克。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Judith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘Jewess’ or ‘woman from Judea’, borne by a Jewish heroine whose story is recorded in the Book of Judith in the Apocrypha. Judith is portrayed as a beautiful widow who delivers her people from the invading Assyrians by gaining the confidence of their commander, Holofernes, and cutting off his head while he is asleep; without their commander, the Assyrians are duly routed. This has been a perennially popular Jewish name. In the English-speaking world it was taken up in the 16th century, having been in occasional use among Gentiles before this: for example, it was borne by a niece of William the Conqueror. It enjoyed great popularity between the 1940s and the 1960s. Today's notable bearers include the American novelist  Judith Krantz  (  b. 1928 ) and the Scottish composer  Judith Weir  (  b. 1954 ).

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Judith Smith was born about 1636, in England, her father, Thomas Smith, was 40 and her mother, Margaret Clarke, was 35. She married Richard Tozer on 3 May 1656, in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 18 June 1683, in Berwick, York, Maine, United States, at the age of 48, and was buried in York, Province of Maine, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Judith Smith est née environ 1636, à Angleterre, son père, Thomas Smith, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Margaret Clarke, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Tozer le 3 mai 1656, à Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 18 juin 1683, à Berwick, York, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à York, Province of Maine, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Judith Smith um 1636 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Smith 40 und ihre Mutter Margaret Clarke 35. Sie hat am 3. Mai 1656 in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Richard Tozer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 18. Juni 1683 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Berwick, York, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in York, Province of Maine, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Judith Smith è nata il circa 1636 nel England, sua padre, Thomas Smith, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Margaret Clarke, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Richard Tozer il 3 maggio 1656, nel Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 18 giugno 1683, nel Berwick, York, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolta a York, Province of Maine, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Judith Smithが約1636年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Thomas Smithは40歳，母Margaret Clarkeは35歳であった。1656年5月3日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクボストンでRichard Tozerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1683年6月18日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークバーウィックで48歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカプロヴィンス オフ メインヨークにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Judith Smith(이)가 대략 1636년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Smith(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Clarke(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1656년 5월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴에서 Richard Tozer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1683년 6월 18일 미국 메인 주 요르크 버윅에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플로빈스 프 맹 요르크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Judith Smith nasceu aproximadamente 1636, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Smith, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Clarke, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Tozer em 3 de maio de 1656, em Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 18 de junho de 1683, em Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 48 anos, e foi sepultada em York, Province of Maine, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Judith Smith родилась около 1636 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Smith было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Clarke, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Tozer 3 мая 1656 г. в местности бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 18 июня 1683 г. в местности бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности ерк, провинц оф меин, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Judith Smith nació aproximadamente 1636, en Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Smith, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Margaret Clarke, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Richard Tozer el 3 de mayo de 1656, en Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 18 de junio de 1683, en Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultada en York, Province of Maine, British Colonial America.
